Title: Multiple transitions to high L-DOPA 4,5-dioxygenase activity reveal molecular pathways to convergent betalain pigmentation in Caryophyllales

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies L-DOPA from tyrosine, enabling betaxanthin production when co-expressed with active DODA; used as substrate-provision module rather than focal discovery gene
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation and reporter output for enzyme activity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BvCYP76AD6 was stably expressed in yeast to hydroxylate tyrosine to L-DOPA. 2. This enabled assessment of DODA-dependent betaxanthin formation. 3. The paper did not investigate CYP76AD6 mechanistic variation as a primary result gene.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous yeast expression combined with fluorescence-based betaxanthin quantification
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively engineered in the URA3 locus of yeast strain yHS023
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is experimentally required in the assay system, but it is not a primary core subject of the Results compared with DODA.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enabling module for synthetic reconstruction of betalain biosynthesis in heterologous platforms
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Medium supplemented with 1 mM tyrosine and 10 mM ascorbic acid; 30°C culture
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: DODA homologues co-expressed in assay strains

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High-activity DODA homologues promote conversion of L-DOPA to betalamic acid and thereby enable elevated betaxanthin/betalain pigmentation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation; fluorescent reporter of betalain pathway activity
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core step linking L-DOPA to betalamic acid formation
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed/key midstream step essential for betalain formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts L-3,4-dihydroxyphenylalanine (L-DOPA) to betalamic acid via 4,5-extradiol ring cleavage
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the direct chromophore precursor required for betaxanthin/betalain production
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point; essential step for betalain biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. High L-DOPA 4,5-dioxygenase activity is polyphyletic in Caryophyllales DODAα. 2. Ancestral reconstruction and yeast assays show high activity evolved independently at least three times from low-activity ancestors. 3. High-activity transitions occurred after gene duplication and support convergent origins of betalain pigmentation. 4. Convergent, divergent, and unique substitutions all contributed to the evolution of high activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast heterologous expression; ancestral sequence resurrection; betaxanthin fluorescence quantification; phylogenetic reconstruction; structural modelling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats multiple extant and ancestral DODAα sequences collectively; most source-species-specific accession numbers for individual focal DODAs are not given in the main text, though sequence accessions are deposited at NCBI (PV487753-PV487786 and PV476478-PV476524).
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Yeast medium with tyrosine and ascorbic acid; 30°C growth; co-expression with BvCYP76AD6
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Quantitative activity differences in yeast may partly reflect protein expression differences rather than intrinsic kinetics
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Sequence datasets from genome/transcriptome assemblies; deposited coding sequences at NCBI PV487753-PV487786 and PV476478-PV476524; structures archived on Zenodo 10.5281/zenodo.15185291
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Yeast cells; naturally betalain-pigmented tissues in Caryophyllales not systematically analyzed here
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Engineered expression after genomic integration into yeast LEU2 locus
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core enzyme target for engineering betalain pigmentation in heterologous systems or crops; should be paired with upstream tyrosine hydroxylation capacity such as CYP76AD-type enzymes

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represents an independently evolved high-activity DODA associated with betalain pigmentation in Stegnospermataceae
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ShDODAα1 belongs to a distinct origin of high DODA activity in Stegnospermataceae. 2. Its high activity is inferred from prior in planta data, although this sequence could not be expressed successfully in yeast in the present study. 3. The Stegnospermataceae lineage shows strong divergence and many unique substitutions relative to other high-activity origins.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior in planta expression evidence cited; phylogenetic placement; ancestral reconstruction of neighboring nodes; structural analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This lineage is unusually divergent, enriched for unique and divergent substitutions, and may also involve insertions/deletions; it lacks significant excess convergence with other origins.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Activity inference partly relies on previous study because yeast expression failed here
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Distinct sequence background may limit transferability of causal substitutions across lineages
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: NCBI deposited sequences within PV ranges; transcriptome/genome-derived sampling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Represents an alternative high-activity DODA scaffold, but unusual divergence may complicate direct engineering transfer

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High-activity DODAα1 supports betalain production and serves as a representative Amaranthaceae high-activity enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BvDODAα1 is used as a representative high-activity DODAα1 from Amaranthaceae. 2. Structural mapping placed known and newly inferred functionally important residues around the predicted binding pocket on BvDODAα1. 3. The Amaranthaceae high-activity lineage shows significant excess molecular convergence with Globular Inclusion high-activity lineages.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast heterologous expression; comparative structural modelling; ancestral/branch convergence analyses
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used extensively as a structural reference in residue mapping figures.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tyrosine + ascorbic acid supplementation in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: NCBI sequence deposit within listed PV accession ranges
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate DODA module for synthetic betalain engineering

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Low/marginal DODA activity relative to DODAα1; used as low-activity comparator for residues controlling high activity
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Same catalytic position but weakly active background
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Marginally catalyzes L-DOPA cleavage relative to high-activity homologues
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as control point in this study; comparator only
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BvDODAα2 is treated as a low-activity DODAα2-like comparator. 2. Prior mutagenesis evidence cited indicates some residues in this weakly active isoform strongly affect L-DOPA 4,5-dioxygenase activity. 3. Sequence divergence between DODAα1-like and DODAα2-like homologues helped identify candidate functional residues.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative sequence/structure analysis in this paper; prior site-directed mutagenesis cited from literature
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a primary experimental target of the present Results, but central comparator for defining amino acid changes associated with high activity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful background scaffold for mutational dissection of activity-determining residues
